behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the HTTP handler’s auth check plus queue handoff suppression. A unit test of only HMAC comparison can miss real regressions where the handler still forwards after a failed check. No real OS/network/LLM dependency is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the webhook handler with a real request object containing an invalid `X-Signature-256` and a queue test double/module instance wired through normal handler code. Assert HTTP 401 and assert zero enqueue/write attempts for that request body/event.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (HTTP handler and persistent queue). Mocking queue persistence too aggressively can hide ordering bugs. Real external network/subprocess is not strictly required.
behaviors.test_description: Use app + real persistent queue adapter (local DB/filesystem). Instrument timestamps/hooks for `queue.write committed` and `response sent`. Send a valid webhook and assert commit occurs before HTTP 200 is emitted.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module interaction between ingress path and async processor scheduling. A single-function unit test can miss accidental `await` on downstream work.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + a deliberately slow downstream consumer in-process. Send a valid webhook and assert HTTP response completes before downstream processing promise resolves.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is a wall-clock latency SLO under concurrency pressure; scheduler/event-loop/runtime behavior matters. Mocked timing can hide real contention and queueing effects, so real process-level execution is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service process with real HTTP endpoint and queue backend. Create concurrent load where earlier events trigger slow downstream work. Measure end-to-end latency of new webhook requests over real HTTP and assert responses stay under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness depends on interaction between handler and persistent dedup/queue state. Unit tests of pure ID-check logic can miss persistence/index/transaction wiring issues.
behaviors.test_description: With handler wired to real persistent queue/dedup store, submit the same valid event ID twice. Assert first request enqueues once, second request does not create a new queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is mapping of queue backpressure signal to HTTP contract. Needs module wiring between queue error and response builder; full system network test is optional, not minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue module to hit capacity and return its canonical "full" error through normal code paths. Send a valid webhook and assert HTTP 503 plus `Retry-After` header is present and correctly formatted.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This validates real queue persistence/config behavior, not just handler logic. Pure unit mocks can pass while real queue storage semantics fail.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue backend with a small capacity (e.g., 2). Write events until limit is reached, then attempt one more write and assert a deterministic capacity-rejection error/state with no overflow write committed.
